libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: Use the 2.0.0 API, not the old 1.x test API: RenderingContext, SkinResourceLoader, enableLogging, setUsingDefaultTemplate, and setDecoration are gone or no longer applicable. Build a minimal skin jar in the test temp directory containing META-INF/maven/site.vm, create a SiteRenderingContext with a SiteModel/publish date and root/site directories, and obtain SiteRenderer from the Plexus test container rather than constructing DefaultSiteRenderer directly. No environment variables, system properties, or Docker services appear necessary.

deterministic_setup.reason: doxia-site-renderer 2.0.0 exposes Maven Artifact in its skin APIs but declares maven-artifact as provided; tests that create a skin context need it explicitly.

DefaultSiteRenderer is normally obtained as a Sisu/Plexus component with injected Doxia, VelocityComponent, ParserModuleManager, and PlexusContainer; upstream 2.0.0 tests use Plexus testing support for this.
The 2.0.0 POM notes parser modules are no longer in doxia-core, so rendering an APT site document requires the module on the test classpath for component discovery.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8134 [Automation] javac compile fails for org.apache.maven.doxia:doxia-site-renderer:2.0.0; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: The issue is primarily an API migration compile failure from 1.9.1 tests to 2.0.0; setup alone will not fix references to removed classes and methods.
Plexus/Sisu component lookup and parser discovery can affect native-image reachability; local Gradle and native-image verification remain authoritative.

Only APT parser support is prepared because it is enough for representative site rendering coverage; add other Doxia parser modules in test code only if the generated tests deliberately cover those formats.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: Meaningful 2.0.0 renderer coverage needs Maven/Plexus-style setup: the renderer is an injected Sisu/Plexus component, its skin API uses Maven Artifact classes that are provided-scope, and non-XHTML Doxia parser modules are not pulled in by the normal scaffold.
